vocab.example: Certain colors like metallic silver can emphasize the aero-dynamic silhouette of a car, making it look faster even when stationary.

vocab.meaning: duy trì sự chuẩn mực/chỉnh tề chuyên nghiệp
vocab.example: Corporate executives often choose navy or charcoal to uphold professional decorum during high-stakes business meetings.

vocab.meaning: phối hợp màu sắc đồng bộ
vocab.example: For some enthusiasts, having a color-coordinated interior that matches the paintwork is the pinnacle of automotive style.
A pearlescent coating provides a multi-dimensional depth that changes depending on the angle of the light.

vocab.meaning: sự biến dạng thị giác (do phản chiếu)
vocab.example: Darker, glossier paints are prone to visual distortion, which makes even the smallest dent look much more prominent.

vocab.meaning: bão hòa thị trường (màu sắc quá phổ biến)
vocab.example: While white and grey saturate the market, choosing a custom green can help a driver express a unique identity.

vocab.meaning: độ trong/rõ nét về mặt thị giác
vocab.example: High-quality clear coats provide excellent optical clarity, making solid colors appear far more rich and profound.

vocab.meaning: phản xạ bức xạ mặt trời
vocab.example: In tropical regions, choosing a white vehicle is a strategic move to deflect solar radiation and keep the cabin temperate.

vocab.meaning: thể hiện vẻ uy quyền/quyền lực
vocab.example: Midnight black is frequently used for government vehicles to convey an authoritative presence on the road.

vocab.meaning: tránh sự bất tiện (về việc vệ sinh/bảo dưỡng)
vocab.example: I would personally choose a champagne tint to shun the inconvenience of having to wash the car every single week.
